description: Known disinformation narrative patterns for automated detection. Sourced from EUvsDisinfo, GEC Pillars report, NATO StratCom COE, and Sprint 1 research.

categories.name: Anti-NATO / Western Alliance
categories.description: Narratives aimed at undermining NATO cohesion and Western alliances

narratives.name: NATO expansion threatens Russia
narratives.description: Claims that NATO eastward expansion is an aggressive, threatening act toward Russia, framing Russia's actions as defensive responses.
example_claims: NATO broke its promise not to expand eastward
NATO is encircling Russia with military bases
NATO expansion provoked the conflict in Ukraine

narratives.name: NATO is obsolete / purposeless
narratives.description: Claims that NATO has no legitimate purpose after the Cold War and exists only to justify military spending and US hegemony.
example_claims: NATO is a Cold War relic that should have been dissolved
NATO exists only to serve American interests
European countries are vassals of the US through NATO

narratives.name: NATO destabilizes regions
narratives.description: Claims that NATO interventions and presence create instability rather than security.
example_claims: Everywhere NATO intervenes, it leaves chaos and destruction
NATO destroyed Libya and created a failed state
NATO's presence in Eastern Europe escalates tensions

categories.name: Anti-EU / European Instability
categories.description: Narratives aimed at undermining EU cohesion and portraying European institutions as failing

narratives.name: EU is collapsing / on verge of breakup
narratives.description: Amplifies any EU disagreement or crisis as evidence of imminent institutional collapse.
example_claims: The EU is falling apart as more countries reject Brussels
Brexit was just the beginning of the EU's dissolution
EU member states are losing sovereignty to unelected bureaucrats

narratives.name: Sanctions hurt Europe more than Russia
narratives.description: Claims that Western sanctions are economically self-destructive and harm European citizens while Russia is unaffected.
example_claims: Sanctions against Russia are destroying the European economy
European citizens are paying the price for anti-Russian sanctions
Russia's economy is growing despite Western sanctions

narratives.name: Migration as EU weapon / threat
narratives.description: Weaponizes migration issues to amplify fears and divisions within the EU.
example_claims: The EU's migration policy is destroying European culture
Mass immigration is leading to the Islamization of Europe
EU forces member states to accept unlimited migrants

categories.name: Ukraine Conflict Revisionism
categories.description: Narratives aimed at justifying Russian actions in Ukraine and undermining Ukrainian sovereignty

narratives.name: Ukraine is a Nazi / fascist state
narratives.description: Labels Ukraine's government or military as Nazi or fascist to delegitimize the state and justify military action.
example_claims: Ukraine is ruled by neo-Nazis who persecute Russian speakers
The Azov Battalion proves Ukraine's Nazi ideology
Denazification of Ukraine is a necessary and legitimate goal

narratives.name: Ukraine is not a real country
narratives.description: Denies Ukrainian sovereignty, national identity, or historical legitimacy as an independent state.
example_claims: Ukraine and Russia are one people artificially divided
Ukraine as a state is an artificial Western creation
Ukrainian language is just a dialect of Russian

narratives.name: Western provocation caused the conflict
narratives.description: Blames Western actions (NATO expansion, EU association, US involvement) for the conflict in Ukraine.
example_claims: The US orchestrated the Maidan coup to install an anti-Russian government
NATO pushed Ukraine into conflict with Russia
Ukraine is a proxy war by the West against Russia

narratives.name: Ukraine bioweapons labs
narratives.description: Claims that US-funded biological research labs in Ukraine are developing bioweapons.
example_claims: The US funded secret bioweapons labs in Ukraine
Russia discovered evidence of biological weapons programs in Ukraine
Pentagon was developing dangerous pathogens near the Russian border

categories.name: Western Hypocrisy / Moral Equivalence
categories.description: Narratives that deflect criticism of Russia by pointing to Western failings, real or exaggerated

narratives.name: Western media is propaganda
narratives.description: Claims that Western mainstream media is state-controlled propaganda, equivalent to or worse than Russian state media.
example_claims: Western media is just propaganda for NATO and the EU
RT provides a much-needed alternative to Western media bias
The West censors Russian media because it fears the truth

narratives.name: Whataboutism / tu quoque
narratives.description: Deflects criticism of Russian actions by pointing to Western equivalents ('What about Iraq? What about Guantanamo?').
example_claims: The West has no right to criticize Russia after what it did in Iraq
Western countries apply double standards when it comes to human rights
Where was the outrage when NATO bombed Yugoslavia?

narratives.name: Western democracy is failing
narratives.description: Amplifies democratic challenges in Western countries to suggest systemic failure.
example_claims: Western democracy is in terminal decline
US elections are just as rigged as those in any authoritarian state
European democracies are descending into chaos

categories.name: Global South Appeals
categories.description: Narratives targeting Global South audiences, positioning Russia as anti-colonial ally

narratives.name: Russia as anti-colonial champion
narratives.description: Positions Russia as ally of the Global South against Western neocolonialism.
example_claims: Russia stands with the Global South against Western neocolonialism
BRICS offers an alternative to Western-dominated institutions
Africa is freeing itself from Western exploitation with Russian help

categories.name: Health Disinformation
categories.description: Narratives leveraging health crises to sow distrust in Western institutions

narratives.name: COVID-19 Western conspiracy
narratives.description: Claims that COVID-19 was created, released, or weaponized by Western actors.
example_claims: COVID-19 was a US bioweapon targeting specific populations
Western vaccines are experimental and dangerous
The pandemic was planned by Western elites to control populations

categories.name: Election Interference Denial
categories.description: Narratives denying or deflecting from documented Russian interference in elections

narratives.name: Russiagate is a hoax
narratives.description: Claims that evidence of Russian interference in Western elections is fabricated.
example_claims: Russian election interference is a conspiracy theory with no evidence
The West blames Russia for its own democratic failures
Russiagate was a hoax designed to demonize Russia

primary_sources: GEC Pillars of Russia's Disinformation and Propaganda Ecosystem (2020)
Stanford IO Election Integrity Partnership reports
